name: Caramelized Banana Mille-Feuille with Espresso Custard, Tonka Bean Air & Gold Leaf Honeycomb
description: A Michelin-level brunch dessert featuring vacuum-compressed caramelized banana layers stacked with espresso custard sheets, paired with tonka bean air, gold-leaf honeycomb shards, and aromatic banana essence.
keywords: banana mille-feuille, espresso custard, tonka bean foam, molecular gastronomy, fine dining brunch, honeycomb candy, modernist dessert

steps.instruction: Prepare vacuum-compressed bananas: Slice bananas into thin planks and seal in a vacuum chamber at full pressure to intensify flavor and density. Rest 10 minutes.
Caramelize banana layers: Heat sugar and glucose to 170°C (338°F). Add butter and salt, then coat banana slices. Lay flat and lightly torch for a glossy finish.
Prepare espresso custard: Heat cream, milk, and espresso. Temper into egg yolks and sugar. Cook to 82°C (180°F), whisk in agar, and set in thin sheets. Chill and cut into precise layers.
Create honeycomb: Cook sugar, honey, and water to 150°C (302°F). Remove from heat, whisk in baking soda, and pour onto a silicone mat. Cool, break into shards, and apply gold leaf.
Prepare tonka bean air: Infuse warm milk with tonka bean, strain, and add lecithin. Use an ultrasonic homogenizer to generate a stable, fine foam.
Optional banana crisp: Flash freeze thin banana slices in liquid nitrogen and shatter into delicate shards.

Plating: Stack alternating banana and custard layers into a mille-feuille. Arrange honeycomb shards vertically. Spoon tonka air alongside. Garnish with cocoa nibs, espresso reduction, and micro mint. Finish with aromatic banana mist if available.

winePairings.name: Espresso Martini
winePairings.notes: Enhances the coffee bitterness and complements the dessert’s layered richness.

winePairings.name: Pedro Ximénez Sherry
winePairings.notes: Deep caramel and raisin notes mirror the banana and honeycomb sweetness.
